theorems.name: Condition for Composition Series
theorems.body:
Let $G$ be a finite group.

Then:
 a normal series $\HH$ for $G$ is a composition series for $G$
 every factor group of $\HH$ is a simple group.
theorems.slogan: A finite group has a composition series, which is a normal series where every factor group is simple.

theorems.name: Finite Group has Composition Series
theorems.body:
Let $G$ be a finite group.

Then $G$ has a composition series.
theorems.slogan: Every finite group has a composition series. A composition series is a finite chain of subgroups from the trivial subgroup to the whole group. Each consecutive subgroup is normal in the next, and the quotient groups are simple. This structure reveals the group's building blocks.

theorems.name: Theorem 1 (Jordan-Hölder Theorem see e.g., \cite {Algebra})
theorems.body: \noindent Let $G$ be a finite group with $G \neq 1 $. Then \begin {enumerate} \item [(i)] $G$ has a composition series. \item [(ii)] The composition factors in a composition series are unique, namely, if $1=N_{r} \leq N_{r-1} \leq \cdots \leq N_{1} \leq N_{0}=G$ and $1=M_{s} \leq M_{s-1}\leq \cdots \leq M_{1} \leq M_{0}=G $ are two composition series for $G$, then $r=s$ and there is some permutation $\pi $ of $\{ 1,2,\dots ,r\}$ such that, $$\frac {M_{\pi (i)}}{M_{\pi (i)+1}} \cong \frac {N_{i}}{N_{i+1}}, \text {for} \, 1 \leq i \leq r. $$ \end {enumerate}
theorems.slogan: Every finite group that is not trivial has a composition series. The composition factors in any such series are unique up to isomorphism. If two composition series exist for the same group, they have the same length. The factors of one series are isomorphic to those of the other, possibly in a different order.

paper.title: Linear Space Data Structures for Finite Groups with Constant Query-time

theorems.name: Theorem
theorems.body:
Let $G$ be a finite group. Then any two composition series of $G$
are equivalent.
theorems.slogan: A finite group has composition series that are all equivalent. This means the length of the series is the same across any choice. The composition factors also appear in the same order up to permutation. This result is known as the Jordan-Holder theorem.

theorems.name: Lemma
theorems.body: Any finite group $G (\neq  {id})$ has a composition series.
theorems.slogan: Every finite group, except the trivial group, has a composition series. A composition series is a chain of subgroups from the group down to the trivial group. Each successive quotient group in this chain is a simple group. This structure reveals important properties about the group's composition.

theorems.name: Jordan-Hölder Theorem
theorems.body:
Let $G$ be a finite group.

Let $\HH_1$ and $\HH_2$ be two composition series for $G$.

Then:
$$\HH_1$ and $\HH_2$ have the same length
 Corresponding factors of $\HH_1$ and $\HH_2$ are isomorphic.
theorems.slogan: For a finite group, any two composition series have the same length. The composition factors from each series are isomorphic in a corresponding order. This holds regardless of the choice of composition series. The result is known as the Jordan-Holder theorem.

theorems.name: Theorem 1 (Jordan-Holder Theorem for finite groups)
theorems.body: Let $G$ be a finite group and let $\{ e\} =G_n \unlhd \cdots G_1\unlhd G_0=G$ and $\{ e\} =H_m \unlhd \cdots H_1\unlhd H_0=G$ be two composition series for $G$. Then for every finite simple group $S$, $|\{ 0\leq i\leq n-1: G_i/G_{i+1}\cong S\} |=|\{ 0\leq i\leq m-1: H_i/H_{i+1}\cong S\} |$. In particular $m=n$ and the sets $\{ G_i/G_{i+1} | \  0 \leq i \leq n-1\} $ and $\{ H_i/H_{i+1} \  | \  0 \leq i \leq m-1\} $ are equal.
theorems.slogan: For a finite group, any two composition series have the same length. The list of composition factors from each series is identical up to permutation. The number of times each finite simple group appears as a factor is the same in both series. This holds regardless of the choice of composition series.

paper.title: Jordan-Holder Theorem for profinite groups and applications

theorems.name: Proposition 1.1
theorems.body: Si $G$ est fini, $G$ poss\`{e}de une suite de Jordan-H\"{o}lder.
theorems.slogan: Every finite group has a Jordan-Hölder series. This series is a composition series of the group. The factors in this series are simple groups. The list of these factors is unique up to permutation and isomorphism.

theorems.name: Proposition A.1
theorems.slogan: The order of a finite group equals the product of the orders of the factors in a composition series. This product is taken over all consecutive quotient groups in the series. The result holds for any composition series of the group. It reflects the Jordan-Hölder theorem's structure.

paper.title: Subgroup Chains and Lagrange Coordinatizations of Finite Permutation Groups

theorems.name: Composition Series of Group of Prime Power Order
theorems.body:
Let $G$ be a group whose identity is $e$, and whose order is a prime power:
$$\order G = p^n, p \in \mathbb P, n \ge 1$

Then $G$ has a composition series:
$$\set e = G_0 \subset G_1 \subset \ldots \subset G_n = G$
such that:
$$\order {G_k} = p^k$

where:
$$G_k \lhd G_{k + 1}$
$$G_{k + 1} / G_k$ is cyclic and of order $p$.
theorems.slogan: Every finite group whose order is a power of a prime has a composition series. The series starts with the trivial subgroup and ends with the whole group. Each subgroup in the chain is normal in the next one. The quotient of each consecutive pair is a cyclic group of prime order.
